title: Continuum 1D Chiral Fermion Hamiltonian
summary: After replacing p-p0 by -i d/dx, the linearized one-dimensional crossing becomes a continuum Hamiltonian H=v integral dx psi* (-i d/dx) psi up to an irrelevant constant shift.
assumptions: The constant energy shift can be dropped for low-energy dynamics.
regime: Long-wavelength effective theory near a one-dimensional crossing.
scope: Lecture One passage from band dispersion to a chiral continuum field.

source_anchors.notes: The constant term is identified as a removable density shift.

representation: Effective continuum Hamiltonian for a single chiral branch.

symbols.meaning: continuum field for the low-energy branch

failure_modes: A single chiral branch is only local; lattice realizations require balancing branches globally.
retrieval_hints: Use when connecting a Fermi crossing to a chiral field theory.
